preamble: The phonetic transformation from a voiced aspirated alveolar plosive (dʰ) to an alveolar trill (r) represents a process of lenition, where a stop consonant weakens in its articulation. This specific sound change is attested in the historical development of Armenian from Proto-Indo-European, occurring in word-medial positions.
phoneticEffects: Lenition, deaspiration, change in manner of articulation from plosive to trill

examples.note: Proto-Indo-European root meaning 'lead' to Armenian 'lead away'.

sources: Source via Google Search grounding (verify before merge)

related.label: Lenition of voiced stop to trill
